name: Celldex Therapeutics, Inc.
business: Celldex Therapeutics is a biopharmaceutical company dedicated to exploring the science of mast cell biology and developing therapeutic antibodies to improve the lives of patients with severe inflammatory, allergic, autoimmune and other devastating diseases. Its drug candidates include monoclonal and bispecific antibodies designed to address mast cell mediated diseases for which available treatments are inadequate.
sellsTo: Patients with mast cell mediated diseases such as chronic spontaneous urticaria, chronic inducible urticaria, prurigo nodularis, and atopic dermatitis

exposures.sentence: Our drug candidates include monoclonal and bispecific antibodies designed to address mast cell mediated diseases for which available treatments are inadequate.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase 3 CSU studies (EMBARQ-CSU1 and EMBARQ-CSU2)
catalysts.sentence: Enrollment to the studies was completed in February 2026 and topline data will be available in Q4 2026.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase 2 PN study
catalysts.sentence: Topline data from the study is expected in summer 2026.

catalysts.event: Topline data from Phase 2 AD study
catalysts.sentence: Topline data from the study is expected in late 2026.

catalysts.event: Data from multiple ascending dose portion and subcutaneous administration of Phase 1 CDX-622 study
catalysts.sentence: Data from the multiple ascending dose portion of the study and subcutaneous administration are anticipated in the third quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: CSU prevalence of 0.5-1.0% of total population or up to approximately 1 to 3 million patients in the United States
tamClaims.sentence: It is one of the most frequent dermatologic diseases with a prevalence of 0.5-1.0% of the total population or up to approximately 1 to 3 million patients in the United States (Weller et al. 2010. Hautarzt. 61(8), Bartlett et al. 2018. DermNet.Org).

tamClaims.claim: CIndU prevalence estimated at 0.5% of total population and overlap in up to 36% of CSU patients
tamClaims.sentence: The prevalence of CIndU is estimated at 0.5% of the total population and is reported to overlap in up to 36% of CSU patients (Weller et al. 2010. Hautarzt. 61(8), Bartlett et al. 2018. DermNet.Org).

tamClaims.claim: Approximately 154,000 PN patients in the US who have undergone treatment within last 12 months, of which approximately 75,000 are biologic-eligible
tamClaims.sentence: Industry sources estimate there are approximately 154,000 patients in the United States with PN who have undergone treatment within the last 12 months and, of these, approximately 75,000 would be biologic-eligible.

tamClaims.claim: AD lifetime prevalence up to 20% of US population; two-thirds of patients on first-line systemic therapy (1.7 million in US) do not achieve complete control
tamClaims.sentence: AD is one of the most common chronic inflammatory skin diseases, with a lifetime prevalence of up to 20% of the US population and a substantial impact on quality of life (Kawakami, et al. 2009).

tamClaims.claim: Two-thirds of patients treated with first line systemic therapy (1.7 million patients in the US) do not achieve complete control of their atopic dermatitis
tamClaims.sentence: Two-thirds of patients treated with first line systemic therapy (1.7 million patients in the US) do not achieve complete control of their atopic dermatitis (Simpson, Bieber, Guttman-Yassky, et al. 2016).
